extraction.one_line_summary: CATANIO Mattia won the 25.4km Tour de Luxembourg ITT by 0.6 seconds over ALMEIDA, who took the race lead with 43 seconds over HERSHEY before the final stage.
tactical_patterns: CATANIO dominated intermediate split but ALMEIDA nearly erased 8.6-second deficit in final half, revealing superior pacing strategy.
HERSHEY lost yellow by 43 seconds despite starting only 4 seconds down, suggesting climbers underestimate ITT losses.
CHELMOS caught 2-minute men despite not winning, indicating aggressive early pacing pays dividends in rolling ITT courses.

rider_signals.CATANIO Mattia: positive: Won stage and jumped to third overall, proving legitimate GC threat with unexpected time trial prowess.
rider_signals.ALMEIDA Xiao: positive: Nearly won ITT and took 47-second chunk from race leader HERSHEY, confirming elite TT ability.
rider_signals.HERSHEY Mark: negative: Lost 43 seconds to ALMEIDA in 25.4km as non-specialist, making yellow jersey vulnerable before final stage.
rider_signals.LATOR Pierre: neutral: Top-five finish shows GT domestiques regain TT form when given protected status instead of mountain support duties.
rider_signals.BALCOMO Lomer: negative: Crashed dramatically mid-ITT yet continued, suggesting equipment or technical error on sporting course.
